model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The fixed-step optimizer changes worked, so the next lever is capacity. A moderately wider, deeper MLP should use the larger effective data budget better without adding much training-loop complexity.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed update budget, the next simple lever is to increase examples per optimizer step again. The current model is still compact enough that a batch-size bump should be a low-risk way to improve gradient quality and make better use of the short schedule.","secondary_modes":["summaries","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last batch-size increase was the largest gain so far, which suggests this fixed-step workload is still benefiting from lower-variance updates and more sample reuse. Pushing batch size one more time is the highest-signal simple follow-up.","secondary_modes":["summaries","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Batch-size scaling has been the dominant source of gains, so the cleanest follow-up is to retune the step size upward for the much larger effective batch. This keeps the current successful setup intact while testing whether optimization is now under-driving the model.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-2","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The higher LR overshot, so the next clean adjustment is to restore the better step size and relax regularization slightly. With the much larger batch and repeated passes through data, the current setup is likely more constrained by weight decay than by optimization stability.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 1","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Dropout was clearly harmful, so this removes it and tests a simpler width profile. With only 256 optimizer updates, a uniform-width MLP may optimize more cleanly than a rapidly expanding stack while still keeping enough capacity for CIFAR-10.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-2","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The recent simplifications both moved away from the best verified region. Restore the previously stronger expanding-width MLP and the slightly heavier weight decay that matched the best 1024-batch run, then continue searching from that recovered baseline.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"At batch 1024 with only 256 optimizer updates, AdamW may benefit from a shorter second-moment horizon than the default. Lowering beta2 is a compact way to make the optimizer respond faster without disturbing the successful schedule, batch size, or architecture.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The beta2 reduction helped, so the next coherent optimizer tweak is to also lower beta1 slightly. With large batches and a short update horizon, reducing momentum can make AdamW adapt faster while staying close to the newly improved setting.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The more responsive AdamW betas improved optimization, which makes a modest LR increase the highest-value nearby test. This stays close to the new best setting while checking whether the earlier LR ceiling was mostly an optimizer-parameter mismatch.","secondary_modes":["indexing","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.9)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.95)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The run keeps improving as the optimizer becomes more responsive. The next nearby test is to shorten AdamW's second-moment memory again, which may better match the very large batch and limited 256-step horizon.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.9)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 10.0)","old":"            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 5.0)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The extra beta2 reduction did not hold, so revert to the better optimizer setting and test a looser clip threshold. With large batches and a tuned AdamW setup, the current clip norm may be suppressing useful updates more than it is preventing instability.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current optimization setup looks near-tuned, so a small loss-level regularization change is a sensible next probe. Mild label smoothing often improves validation cross-entropy directly, especially when training with large batches and a short fixed update budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Label smoothing moved validation loss in the wrong direction, so the highest-value next step is to remove it and recover the stronger loss configuration before testing anything more aggressive. Cross-entropy itself is the target metric here, and the current optimizer setup already seems well tuned without extra target smoothing.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Most nearby optimizer-only tweaks have been explored, so the remaining high-value probe is to remove batch normalization entirely. With very large batches, AdamW, and only a few hundred updates, BN may be adding more inductive bias and train/eval mismatch than useful stabilization in this pure MLP.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Removing batch normalization was decisively harmful, so the priority is to restore the strong normalization-enabled baseline before using the final step on a smaller nearby tweak. The current search region is narrow enough that carrying forward a clearly bad architectural change is not worthwhile.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.8, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.95)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Lowering beta1 from 0.9 to 0.85 produced one of the strongest late-stage gains, while further beta2 reduction did not help. With one step left, the best nearby probe is to continue in that direction and make AdamW a bit more responsive again without changing the successful batch size, schedule, or architecture.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}
